# audobject

{{ tests }} {{ coverage }} {{ docs }} {{ python-versions }} {{ license }}

Generic interface for serializing objects to [yaml].

Have a look at the [installation] and [usage] instructions.

% badges images and links:

[installation]: https://audeering.github.io/audobject/install.html
[usage]: https://audeering.github.io/audobject/usage.html
[yaml]: https://yaml.org/

variables.name: audobject.importlib

modules.name: audobject.resolver
documentation:
Resolver classes.

{class}`audobject.resolver.Base` can be used to control
how an object of certain type is represented in YAML.

By default,
the following types are supported:

- `bool`
- `datetime.datetime`
- `dict`
- `float`
- `int`
- `list`
- `None`
- `Object`
- `str`

For any other type,
it is not ensured that an object can be properly reconstructed
and a warning will be raised.
It is recommended to use a special resolver in those cases.

To apply a resolver,
register it using the {func}`audobject.init_decorator`, e.g.:

```
import audobject


class MyObjectWithTuple(audobject.Object):

    @audobject.init_decorator(
        resolvers={
            't': audobject.resolver.Tuple,
        }
    )

    def __init__(
            self,
            t: tuple,
    ):
        self.t = t
```

To write a custom resolver derive from
{class}`audobject.resolver.Base`, e.g.:

```
from datetime import timedelta


class DeltaResolver(audobject.resolver.Base):

    def decode(self, value: dict) -> timedelta:
        return timedelta(
            days=value['days'],
            seconds=value['seconds'],
            microseconds=value['microseconds'],
        )

    def encode(self, value: timedelta) -> dict:
        return {
            'days': value.days,
            'seconds': value.seconds,
            'microseconds': value.microseconds,
        }

    def encode_type(self):
        return dict
```

functions.name: audobject.core.decorator.init_decorator
documentation:
Decorator for `__init__` function of {class}`audobject.Object`.

If your class expects an argument `a`
which is passed on as an argument to a class `C`
or to a dictionary with key `a`,
and stored under `self.b`,
you can borrow the argument from `b`
by setting `borrow = {'a': 'b'}`.
This will store `a` in the YAML representation,
even though your class actually does not
have a corresponding attribute `self.a`.

Arguments listed in `hidden` are not serialized to YAML.
Note that objects you borrow attributes from, are also treated as
hidden arguments.

If a dictionary of {class}`audobject.ValueResolver` is passed,
matching attributes will be encoded / decoded
using the according {class}`audobject.ValueResolver`.

Args:

: borrow: borrowed attributes
  hide: hidden attributes
  resolvers: dictionary with resolvers

Raises:

: RuntimeError: if arguments are not assigned to attributes of same name

functions.name: audobject.core.utils.create_class_key
documentation:
Create class key.

Convert class into a string that encodes
package, module, class name and possibly version.
Package name is omitted if it matches the module.

Examples:
- $audeer.core.version.LooseVersion
- $audeer.core.version.LooseVersion==1.18.0
- $PyYAML:yaml.loader.Loader

documentation: Load class.

documentation: Load module.

functions.name: audobject.core.utils.get_object
documentation: Create object from arguments without calling `__init__()`.

functions.name: audobject.core.utils.is_class
documentation: Check if value is a class.

functions.name: audobject.core.utils.split_class_key
documentation:
Split class key into package, module, class and version.

Expects a key in the format output by create_class_key().
If package is not encoded,
the module name is returned.
If version is not encoded,
None is returned.
Leading $ can be omitted.

classes.name: audobject.core.define.PackageMismatchWarnLevel

class_variables.name: audobject.core.define.PackageMismatchWarnLevel.SILENT
documentation: no warnings

class_variables.name: audobject.core.define.PackageMismatchWarnLevel.STANDARD
documentation: only warn when installed version is older

class_variables.name: audobject.core.define.PackageMismatchWarnLevel.VERBOSE
documentation: warn when versions do not match

Controls verbosity for package mismatch.

A package mismatch might occur
if the current installed version of a package
does not match the version an object
was generated with.

Set via {class}`audobject.config`.

classes.name: audobject.core.define.SignatureMismatchWarnLevel

class_variables.name: audobject.core.define.SignatureMismatchWarnLevel.SILENT
documentation: no warnings

class_variables.name: audobject.core.define.SignatureMismatchWarnLevel.STANDARD
documentation: warn when arguments are removed

class_variables.name: audobject.core.define.SignatureMismatchWarnLevel.VERBOSE
documentation: show all warnings

Controls verbosity for signature mismatch.

A signature mismatch might occur
if an object is loaded from a different package version
it was generated with.

Set via {class}`audobject.config`.

functions.name: audobject.core.api.from_dict
documentation:
Create object from dictionary.

Args:

: d: dictionary representing the object
  root: if dictionary was read from a file, set to source directory
  auto_install: install missing packages needed to create the object
  override_args: override arguments in `d` or

  > default values of hidden arguments

Returns:

: object

Raises:
: RuntimeError: if a mandatory argument of the object

  : is missing in the dictionary `d`

functions.name: audobject.core.api.from_yaml
documentation:
Create object from YAML file.

Args:
    path_or_stream: file path or stream
    auto_install: install missing packages needed to create the object
    override_args: override arguments in the YAML file or
        default values of hidden arguments

Returns:
    object

functions.name: audobject.core.api.from_yaml_s
documentation:
Create object from YAML string.

Args:
    yaml_string: YAML string
    auto_install: install missing packages needed to create the object
    override_args: override arguments in the YAML string or
        default values of hidden arguments

Returns:
    object

classes.name: audobject.core.object.Object

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.arguments
documentation:
Returns arguments that are serialized.

Returns:
    Dictionary of arguments and their values.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: if arguments are found that are not assigned to
        attributes of the same name

Examples:
    >>> import audobject.testing
    >>> o = audobject.testing.TestObject('test', point=(1, 1))
    >>> o.arguments
    {'name': 'test', 'point': (1, 1)}

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.borrowed_arguments
documentation:
Returns borrowed arguments.

Returns:
    Dictionary with borrowed arguments.

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.hidden_arguments
documentation:
Returns hidden arguments.

Returns:
    List with names of hidden arguments.

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.id
documentation:
Object identifier.

The ID of an object ID is created from its non-hidden arguments.

Returns:
    object identifier

Examples:
    >>> class Foo(Object):
    ...    def __init__(self, bar: str):
    ...        self.bar = bar
    >>> foo1 = Foo('I am unique!')
    >>> foo1.id
    '893df240-babe-d796-cdf1-c436171b7a96'
    >>> foo2 = Foo('I am different!')
    >>> foo2.id
    '9303f2a5-bfc9-e5ff-0ffa-a9846e2d2190'
    >>> foo3 = Foo('I am unique!')
    >>> foo1.id == foo3.id
    True

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.is_loaded_from_dict
documentation:
Check if object was loaded from a dictionary.

Returns `True`
if object was initialized
from a dictionary,
e.g. after loading it from a YAML file.

Returns:
: `True` if object was loaded from a dictionary,

  : otherwise `False`

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.resolvers
documentation:
Return resolvers.

Returns:
    Dictionary with resolvers.

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.short_id
documentation:
Short object identifier.

The short ID consists of eight characters
and is created from its non-hidden arguments.

Returns:
    short object identifier

Examples:
    >>> class Foo(Object):
    ...    def __init__(self, bar: str):
    ...        self.bar = bar
    >>> foo1 = Foo('I am unique!')
    >>> foo1.id
    '893df240-babe-d796-cdf1-c436171b7a96'
    >>> foo1.short_id
    '171b7a96'
    >>> foo2 = Foo('I am different!')
    >>> foo2.short_id
    '6e2d2190'
    >>> foo3 = Foo('I am unique!')
    >>> foo1.short_id == foo3.short_id
    True

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.to_dict
documentation:
Converts object to a dictionary.

Includes items from {attr}`audobject.Object.arguments`.
If an argument has a resolver, its value is encoded.
Usually, the object can be re-instantiated using
{meth}`audobject.Object.from_dict`.
However, if `flatten=True`, this is not possible.

Args:

: include_version: add version to class name
  flatten: flatten the dictionary
  root: if file is written to disk, set to target directory

Returns:

: dictionary that represent the object

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> import audobject.testing
  >>> o = audobject.testing.TestObject('test', point=(1, 1))
  >>> o.to_dict(include_version=False)
  {'$audobject.core.testing.TestObject': {'name': 'test', 'point': [1, 1]}}
  >>> o.to_dict(flatten=True)
  {'name': 'test', 'point.0': 1, 'point.1': 1}
  ```

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.to_yaml
documentation:
Save object to YAML file.

Args:
    path_or_stream: file path or stream
    include_version: add version to class name

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.to_yaml_s
documentation:
Convert object to YAML string.

Args:
    include_version: add version to class name

Returns:
    YAML string

Examples:
    >>> import audobject.testing
    >>> o = audobject.testing.TestObject('test', point=(1, 1))
    >>> print(o.to_yaml_s(include_version=False))
    $audobject.core.testing.TestObject:
      name: test
      point:
      - 1
      - 1

methods.name: audobject.core.object.Object.__eq__
documentation: Check if two objects are equal.

Base class for objects that can be serialized to YAML.

Examples:
    >>> class Foo(Object):
    ...    def __init__(self, bar: str):
    ...        self.bar = bar
    >>> foo = Foo('hello object!')
    >>> print(foo)
    $audobject.core.object.Foo:
      bar: hello object!

classes.name: audobject.core.testing.TestObject
documentation:
Test object.

Args:

: name: object name
  point: point with two dimensions
  date: date, if `None` initialized with current date
  kwargs: additional variables

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> from datetime import datetime
  >>> foo = TestObject('test', pi=3.1416)
  >>> print(foo)
  $audobject.core.testing.TestObject:
    name: test
    point:
    - 0
    - 0
    pi: 3.1416
  ```

documentation: Number of keys in dictionary.

methods.name: audobject.core.dictionary.Dictionary.__setitem__
documentation: Set value at key in dictionary.

Dictionary that can be serialized to YAML.

Args:
    **kwargs: items

Examples:
    >>> # create dictionary
    >>> d = Dictionary(foo='foo', bar='bar')
    >>> # set item
    >>> d['foo'] = 'FOO'
    >>> # get item
    >>> d['foo']
    'FOO'
    >>> # add item
    >>> d['new'] = 1.234
    >>> print(d)
    $audobject.core.dictionary.Dictionary:
      foo: FOO
      bar: bar
      new: 1.234
    >>> d2 = Dictionary(foo='Foo', none=None)
    >>> d.update(d2)
    >>> print(d)
    $audobject.core.dictionary.Dictionary:
      foo: Foo
      bar: bar
      new: 1.234
      none: null

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.Base.root
documentation:
Root folder.

Returns root folder when object is serialized to or from a file,
otherwise `None` is returned.

Returns:

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.Base.decode
documentation:
Decode value.

Takes the encoded value and converts it back to its original type.

Args:
    value: value to decode

Returns:
    decoded value

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.Base.encode
documentation:
Encode value.

The type of the returned value must be one of:

- `bool`
- `datetime.datetime`
- `dict`
- `float`
- `int`
- `list`
- `None`
- `Object`
- `str`

Args:

: value: value to encode

Returns:

: encoded value

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.Base.encode_type
documentation:
Return encoded type.

Returns:
    encoded type

Abstract resolver class.

Implement for arguments that are not one of:

- `bool`
- `datetime.datetime`
- `dict`
- `float`
- `int`
- `list`
- `None`
- `Object`
- `str`

classes.name: audobject.core.resolver.FilePath

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.FilePath.decode
documentation:
Decode file path.

If object is read from a file,
this will convert a relative file path
to an absolute path by expanding it
with the source directory.

Args:
    value: relative file path

Returns:
    expanded file path

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.FilePath.encode
documentation:
Encode file path.

If object is written to a file,
this will convert a file path
to a path that is relative to the
target directory.

Args:
    value: original file path

Returns:
    relative file path

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.FilePath.encode_type
documentation:
Return encoded type.

Returns:
    encoded type

File path resolver.

Turns file path to a relative path
when object is serialized to a file
and expands it again during reading.

Examples:
    >>> resolver = FilePath()
    >>> resolver._object_root_ = '/some/root'  # usually set by object
    >>> value = '/some/where/else'
    >>> encoded_value = resolver.encode(value)
    >>> encoded_value  # doctest: +SKIP
    '../where/else'
    >>> resolver.decode(encoded_value)  # doctest: +SKIP
    '/some/where/else'

Decode (lambda) function.

Args:

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.Function.encode_type

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.Function.get_source
documentation:
Obtain source code of (lambda) function.

Retrieving the source of a lambda function can become tricky,
see the following link for detailed discussion:

http://xion.io/post/code/python-get-lambda-code.html

Args:
    func: function object

Returns:
    source code

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.Function._get_short_lambda_source
documentation:
Return the source of a (short) lambda function.

If it's impossible to obtain, returns None.

Original code:
https://gist.github.com/Xion/617c1496ff45f3673a5692c3b0e3f75a

Function resolver.

Encodes source code of function and
dynamically evaluates it when the value is decoded again.

Note that a decoded function
might raise a {class}`NameError`,
if it relies on objects or functions
that are not defined or imported
inside the function.
For instance,
the following example will raise an error
since `plus_1()` relies on `_plus_1()`,
which is defined outside the function:

```python
def _plus_1(x):
    return x + 1

def plus_1(x):
    return _plus_1(x)  # calls local function -> not serializable

resolver = Function()
encoded_value = resolver.encode(plus_1)
del _plus_1
decoded_value = resolver.decode(encoded_value)
decoded_value(1)
```

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> resolver = Function()
  >>> def func(x):
  ...     return x * 2
  >>> func(5)
  10
  >>> encoded_value = resolver.encode(func)
  >>> encoded_value
  'def func(x):\n    return x * 2\n'
  >>> decoded_value = resolver.decode(encoded_value)
  >>> decoded_value(5)
  10
  ```

classes.name: audobject.core.resolver.Tuple

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.Tuple.decode
documentation:
Decodes `list` as `tuple`.

methods.name: audobject.core.resolver.Tuple.encode_type
documentation:
Return encoded type.

Returns:
    encoded type

Tuple resolver.

Encodes tuple as a list.

Examples:
    >>> resolver = Tuple()
    >>> value = (1, 'a')
    >>> value
    (1, 'a')
    >>> encoded_value = resolver.encode(value)
    >>> encoded_value
    [1, 'a']
    >>> decoded_value = resolver.decode(encoded_value)
    >>> decoded_value
    (1, 'a')

Type resolver.

Encodes type as a string.

Examples:
    >>> resolver = Type()
    >>> value = str
    >>> value
    <class 'str'>
    >>> encoded_value = resolver.encode(value)
    >>> encoded_value
    'str'
    >>> decoded_value = resolver.decode(encoded_value)
    >>> decoded_value
    <class 'str'>

classes.name: audobject.core.parameter.Parameter

methods.name: audobject.core.parameter.Parameter.__contains__
documentation: Check if parameter is in parameter object.

methods.name: audobject.core.parameter.Parameter.set_value
documentation:
Sets a new value.

Applies additional checks, e.g. if value is of the expected type.

Args:
    value: new value

Raises:
    TypeError: if value has an invalid type
    ValueError: if value is not in choices

methods.name: audobject.core.parameter.Parameter._check_value
documentation: Check if value matches expected type.

Single parameter.

A parameter steers the behaviour of a an object (e.g. tuning a model).
It has a specific type and default value, possibly one of a set of choices.
It is possible to bound a parameter to a specific version (range).

Args:

: value_type: data type, one of

  : (`None`, `str`, `int`, `float`, `bool`)
    or a `list` or `dict` of those

  description: description
  value: value, if `None` set to `default_value`
  default_value: default value
  choices: list with choices
  version: string defining in which versions the parameter is used

  > (e.g. `'>=1.0.0,<2.0.0,!=1.5.0'`),
  > `None` matches any version

Raises:

: TypeError: if value has an invalid type
  ValueError: if value is not in choices

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> foo = Parameter(
  ...     value_type=str,
  ...     description='some parameter',
  ...     default_value='bar',
  ...     choices=['bar', 'Bar', 'BAR'],
  ...     version='>=1.0.0,<2.0.0',
  ... )
  >>> # check version
  >>> '1.5.0' in foo
  True
  >>> '2.0.0' in foo
  False
  >>> # get/set value
  >>> foo.value
  'bar'
  >>> foo.set_value('Bar')
  >>> foo.value
  'Bar'
  >>> # set invalid value
  >>> try:
  ...     foo.set_value('par')
  ... except ValueError as ex:
  ...     print(ex)
  Invalid value 'par', expected one of ['bar', 'Bar', 'BAR'].
  ```

methods.name: audobject.core.parameter.Parameters.filter_by_version

Filter parameters by version.

Returns a subset including only those parameters that match a given

Creates path from parameters.

Args:
    delimiter: delimiter character
    include: list of parameters to include
    exclude: list of parameters to exclude

methods.name: audobject.core.parameter.Parameters.__call__
documentation: Return parameters as dictionary.

List of parameters.

Args:

: kwargs: {class}`audobject.Parameter` objects

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> # create parameter
  >>> foo = Parameter(
  ...     value_type=str,
  ...     description='foo',
  ... )
  >>> # create list of parameters
  >>> params = Parameters(foo=foo)
  >>> # get / set parameter value
  >>> params.foo = 'bar'
  >>> params.foo
  'bar'
  >>> # add another parameter to list
  >>> pi = Parameter(
  ...     value_type=float,
  ...     description='mathematical constant',
  ...     value=3.14159265359,
  ... )
  >>> params['pi'] = pi
  >>> print(params)
  Name  Value          Default  Choices  Description            Version
  ----  -----          -------  -------  -----------            -------
  foo   bar            None     None     foo                    None
  pi    3.14159265359  None     None     mathematical constant  None
  >>> # convert to dictionary
  >>> params()
  {'foo': 'bar', 'pi': 3.14159265359}
  ```
